description: The goToStep routing action (jump execution to the step with a given stepId). Only PASS/SKIPPED permutations are expressible as a fixture (the combined suite asserts 0 fails): a forward jump over a middle step, and a goToStep on a guard-skipped step's onSkip. The FAIL-path behaviors — backward-jump re-runs (visit===2), unknown-target fail-safe stop, the infinite-loop visit cap — are asserted in focused it() tests in core-core.test.js, since they resolve to FAIL or need a counter file. Cross-platform via `node -e`.

tests.description: A passing step routes goToStep to a later step, skipping the intermediate step entirely (the intermediate step never runs and produces no report). The jumped-to step and the source step both PASS.

steps.description: Passes, then jumps forward to 'goToStep-forward-target', skipping the middle step.
Jumped over by the forward goToStep — this step never runs.
The forward-jump target; runs and passes.

tests.description: A guard-`if`-false step is SKIPPED, and its onSkip routes goToStep to a later step, skipping the intermediate step. The skipped source step records SKIPPED; the jumped-to step PASSes.

steps.description: Guard is always false, so this step is SKIPPED; its onSkip jumps to the target.
Jumped over by the onSkip goToStep — this step never runs.
The onSkip-jump target; runs and passes.
